description_long: This object is a delicate, pale green flower with four broad, rounded petals arranged in a roughly radial pattern. The petals are thin and slightly translucent, with subtle vein textures visible across their surfaces. The flower is supported by a single, slender, and slightly curved black stem that extends downward from the center. There are no visible leaves or additional floral structures attached to the stem. The overall appearance is simple and elegant, with the petals forming a flat, open shape reminiscent of a clover or water lily. The coloration is uniform, with a soft, matte finish that gives the flower a natural and understated look.
description: A pale green flower with four broad, rounded petals and a slender black stem, arranged in a flat, open radial pattern. The surface is matte and subtly veined.
description_view_0: The flower is seen from above, showing all four petals spread out symmetrically around the central stem.
description_view_1: A low side view highlights the flatness of the petals and the gentle upward curve at their edges.
description_view_2: The flower is viewed from the opposite side, with the stem appearing more vertical and the petal arrangement still clearly visible.
description_view_3: A slightly angled view from above, showing the radial symmetry and the subtle curvature of the petals.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, with each petal spreading outward from a central point. The arrangement is symmetrical, and the petals do not overlap significantly, forming a flat, open whorl.
